channel.title: Pharma AI & Data Watch
channel.description: Curated AI, pharma, and regulatory science intelligence — updated weekly.

item.title: Is Kaposi's sarcoma the end of the OX40/OX40L axis in atopic dermatitis?
item.description: The OX40/OX40L axis has shown durable activity in clinical development for atopic dermatitis with strong biological rationale, but questions have emerged regarding whether monotherapy benefits are sufficient compared to established and emerging treatment options. The development of this therapeutic

item.title: Systematic optimization of PD-L1 and CLDN18.2 CAR-T designs identifies a bicistronic dual-target, double-CD3ζ architecture with enhanced antitumor activity in gastric cancer
item.description: Researchers systematically optimized chimeric antigen receptor T-cell (CAR-T) designs targeting PD-L1 and CLDN18.2 to identify an enhanced architecture using a bicistronic construct with dual signaling domains (double-CD3ζ) for treating gastric cancer. This optimized dual-target, double-CD3ζ CAR-T d

item.title: Antony pegg: introducing coldfront: seamlessly uniting oltp, analytics and AI workloads on postgresql
item.description: Our team is excited to announce pgEdge ColdFront v1.0.0-beta1: open-source, transparent data tiering for PostgreSQL that unites OLTP, analytics and AI workloads, with no application code changes required. The headline feature: a fully writable cold tier. Jimm…

item.title: Why transparent AI matters for enterprise trust
item.description: Black box AI risks slowing adoption as enterprises demand transparency, governance and accountability.

item.title: What is artificial intelligence (AI)?
item.description: * AI’s impact comes from its breadth: the same pattern-recognition capabilities now power everything from fraud detection and medical imaging to personalization and content generation. * AI capability is advancing faster than AI reliability, making evaluatio…

item.title: France advances europe’s AI future with nvidia technologies
item.description: France laid out plans to advance local AI — from new AI factories and national compute capacity to open frontier models and industrial platforms. Now, that AI infrastructure is coming online. AI agents are running in production, startups are deploying applica…

item.title: Some states are forging ahead with AI regulations
item.description: Six months after President Donald Trump warned states not to regulate artificial intelligence, they are increasingly doing just that. Congress has stalled on producing federal regulation of artificial intelligence as states forge ahead and scrutinize how chat…

item.title: How inova modernized its data architecture to prepare for AI apps
item.description: Inova went through a data modernization effort to prepare the health system's data foundation to support the rollout of capabilities such as AI agents and ambient scribes.

item.title: Just 30% of financial firms use AI for customer retention
item.description: Enterprise artificial intelligence has moved past the pilot stage, but there are some challenges when it comes to gaining wider acceptance. The PYMNTS Intelligence report “Financial Services Pulls Ahead in the Enterprise AI Race” was based on a survey of 60 s…

item.title: Uae’s push towards agentic AI raises stakes for governance and accountability
item.description: As the UAE accelerates plans to embed autonomous AI into government services, experts warn that governance frameworks must evolve from policy documents into operational controls that ensure transparency, accountability and trust

item.title: Dpp readiness: when and how brands and suppliers can get started
item.description: This article outlines when and how brands and suppliers can prepare for the Digital Product Passport (DPP), a new EU regulation for product data.

item.title: Beyond oncogenesis: the unexplored benefits of viruses in cancer immunity
item.description: The study of tumour virology has traditionally focused on oncogenic viruses and their associated mechanisms of cancer initiation and promotion. Yet certain viral exposures can be beneficial by enhancing antitumour immunity. In this Review, Wang et al. explore…

item.title: Roachfest london 2026: the database as competitive asset
item.description: SPONSORED POST: Operate without fear. Build with confidence. Adapt to the AI era

item.title: Nasa picks eric schmidt's rocket company for mars mission, setting up a race with spacex | techcrunch
item.description: Relativity Space—a rocket maker acquired by former Google executive chair Eric Schmidt last year after stumbling on the path to orbit—might just beat SpaceX to Mars.

item.title: OpenAI is bringing on some big guns in the lead-up to its IPO
item.description: OpenAI is bulking up before its IPO, landing Transformer co-inventor Noam Shazeer from Google DeepMind and former Trump AI policy official Dean Ball in the same week.

item.title: EpiMII: Structure-Aware Graph Neural Networks for MHC-II Epitope Generation
item.description: EpiMII is a computational tool utilizing structure-aware graph neural networks to predict and generate major histocompatibility complex class II (MHC-II) epitopes. The approach integrates structural information through graph-based machine learning to improve epitope identification, supporting immuno

item.title: Advances in microneedle design for the delivery of drugs, proteins, and cells in the treatment of hypertrophic scars
item.description: This review examines advances in microneedle design for transdermal and intradermal delivery of drugs, proteins, and cells as therapeutic approaches for hypertrophic scar treatment. Microneedle technology offers minimally invasive delivery mechanisms that can enhance drug penetration and localized t

item.title: Fulcrumsec targets novo nordisk, leaks clinical and research data
item.description: FulcrumSec leaked data stolen from Novo Nordisk, claiming to have exfiltrated 1.3TB, including clinical records and AI research assets. On June 15, 2026, a data-theft extortion group calling itself FulcrumSec began leaking files from Novo Nordisk, the Danish …

item.title: Do we want to lead or lag in life sciences?
item.description: Life sciences leadership depends on strong ecosystems that connect science, capital and scale. Europe must do far more to support and incentivize innovation

item.title: Cygnvs launches command center for crises caused by a company’s own AI
item.description: Cyber resilience company CYGNVS Inc. today launched its AI Incident Command Center, a platform built to help organizations manage operational crises caused by their own artificial intelligence deployments. The product extends CYGNVS’ existing out-of-band inci…

item.title: 2026 top trends in academic libraries: a review of the trends and issues | planning and review committee | college & research libraries news
item.description: 2026 Top Trends in Academic Libraries: A Review of the Trends and Issues

item.title: Guideai health corp. announces closing of business combination, subscription receipt offering, and filing of final prospectus
item.description: Not for distribution to U.S. Newswire services or for dissemination in the United States. Any failure to comply with this restriction may constitute a violation of U.S. Securities laws. BOSTON, June 16, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — GuideAI Health Corp. (formerly, …

item.title: Towards autonomous medical artificial intelligence agents
item.description: A large language model artificial intelligence agent operating in a sandboxed electronic health record system can autonomously take patient histories, order tests, interpret findings, diagnose conditions and propose treatments, outperforming experienced …

item.title: “digital colonialism”: u.s. demands to access africans’ data raise privacy, sovereignty concerns
item.description: The United States is requiring access to health data as part of lifesaving aid deals with African countries. The U.S. says the data will be aggregated and anonymized, but privacy experts fear the information could be misused or exploited.

item.title: Night owls have 79% higher risk of poor heart health and 16% higher risk of heart attack, per 300,000-person study
item.description: A 300,000-person study in JAHA found night owls had 79% higher risk of poor heart health and 16% higher heart attack and stroke risk. Here's what it means and what to do.

item.title: Analysis of 173,303 exomes and genomes in the pakistan genome resource
item.description: The Pakistan Genome Resource compiles biobank data from 173,303 individuals with high familial relatedness, broadening the catalogue of human genetic variation and establishing a population-specific genomic reference for Pakistan.

item.title: System76 boss reckons he can liberate the entire pc stack... just give him another 15 years
item.description: Bootstrapped Linux box-botherer flogs new Thelio kit, talks up COSMIC, and politely declines to bolt AI onto everything

item.title: FDA Approves First Single-Dose Generic Treatment for Influenza
item.description: The U.S. Food and Drug Administration today approved the first generic of Xofluza (baloxavir marboxil) tablets, the first single-dose treatment for acute uncomplicated influenza and prophylaxis in patients 5 years of age and older.

item.title: STAT+: FDA appears open to Moderna’s flu vaccine ahead of adcomm
item.description: UniQure will submit Huntington's therapy for approval, Eli Lilly buys 4E Therapeutics, and more biotech news

item.title: STAT+: Following dispute with FDA, UniQure is cleared to submit Huntington’s treatment for approval
item.description: The FDA has reversed its opposition to a closely watched experimental treatment for Huntington’s disease, clearing a path for its maker.

item.title: NEA’s Tiffany Luck says enterprises are still figuring out their AI ROI
item.description: Tokenmaxxing was the hottest trend in Silicon Valley earlier this year, with CEOs encouraging employees to push AI usage as far as it would go. Then the bill came due. Uber reportedly blew through its annual AI budget in a few months, some companies cut Claude licenses

item.title: NEA’s Tiffany Luck on AI IPOs, personal agents, and the ROI reckoning
item.description: Tokenmaxxing was the hottest trend in Silicon Valley earlier this year, with CEOs encouraging employees to push AI usage as far as it would go. Then the bill came due. Uber reportedly blew through its annual AI budget in a few months, some companies cut Claude licenses

item.title: Google bets on Gemini to reinvent the smart home speaker
item.description: Google is betting generative AI can breathe new life into the smart speaker. The company's new $99.99 Google Home Speaker replaces the rigid commands of the Google Assistant era with more conversational Gemini interactions.

item.title: Machine learning-driven identification of virulence determinants in borrelia burgdorferi associated with human dissemination
item.description: Author summary Lyme disease, caused by Borrelia burgdorferi (Bb), afflicts over 470,000 Americans annually, yet predicting which infections will progress from localized to disseminated disease remains a critical clinical challenge. Despite decades of research…

item.title: Openai’s balance sheet remains the most mysterious—and consequential—in business
item.description: A combination of blogger Ed Zitron and the Financial Times reported that OpenAI’s 2025 financials look, well, pretty rough.

item.title: ‘agrivoltaics’ can both power AI data centres and increase food production — new study
item.description: In Canada, agrivoltaics could produce enough electricity to eliminate the need for fossil fuels on the grid entirely, using less than one per cent of the country’s agriculture land.

item.title: Bernie sanders unveils plan to give the public direct ownership of AI companies
item.description: As artificial intelligence companies reshape the economy and race toward trillion-dollar valuations, Sen. Bernie Sanders is proposing a sweeping transfer of wealth and power from the industry to the American public. The legislation, shown first to The Associa…

item.title: Bernie sanders unveils plan for public ownership of AI companies
item.description: Sen. Bernie Sanders is proposing a plan to give Americans ownership stakes in the country’s largest artificial intelligence companies. The legislation shown first to The Associated Press would impose a one-time 50% tax on the stock of major AI companies and p…

item.title: Vibe-decoding the white house-anthropic fight over fable
item.description: Hello and welcome to Regulator, an email for Verge subscribers about technology, politics, and what happens when science crashes headlong into self-interest. Not a subscriber? Sign up here today! Got the scoop on a petty feud that's going to somehow fundament…

item.title: Gartner trends signal a governance gap as AI agents take on decisions
item.description: Your AI agents are already making decisions faster than your governance can keep up. Gartner's newest trend report pinpoints exactly where that gap opens, and what data leaders can do before the next agent acts unsupervised. Source: Gartner Trends Signal a Go…

item.title: The case against building your own agent platform
item.description: You know the meeting. The board wants an AI agent strategy by end of quarter. Someone on the leadership team has read a McKinsey report. You’ve been voluntold to build the platform. The slide deck says “AI-native.” The acceptance criteria are vague. Somebody …

item.title: A 22-year-old just raised $11.6 million to read women’s hidden hormone signals
item.description: A 22-year-old Stanford grad raised $11.6M to build a wearable that infers hormone levels from your wrist. Can inference replace a blood test?

item.title: 12 top machine learning use cases and business applications
item.description: Machine learning applications in the era of GenAI and AI agents are increasing the efficiency and accuracy of everything from decision-making to maintenance to service delivery.

item.title: Lxnet: a lightweight cnn for lung disease classification from chest x-ray with xai-based interpretability
item.description: The diagnosis of lung diseases such as pneumonia and tuberculosis remains a major global health challenge, especially in resource-limited regions. Artificial Intelligence (AI) has shown strong potential in analyzing Chest X-Rays (CXR) for accurate and timely …

item.title: Adverse events following hpv vaccine reported to the vaccine adverse event reporting system
item.description: Background Vaccination against high-risk HPV types is a key preventive measure. However, concerns regarding vaccine safety may hinder vaccination efforts. This study aims to evaluate adverse events (AEs) reported in the Vaccine Adverse Event Reporting System …

item.title: Proscia launches fifth generation of concentriq, bringing the latest advancements in AI to pathologists and scientists
item.description: Built on a new AI-native architecture, Concentriq delivers clinically and scientifically grounded intelligence to decisions throughout drug discovery, development, and case review PHILADELPHIA, June 17, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) — Proscia® today introduced the Fi…

item.title: Tyrosine is in millions of supplement products. a 270,000-person study links higher levels to shorter lifespan in men
item.description: Study of 270,000+ people in Aging-US: men with higher tyrosine levels may live nearly a year less. The amino acid is a common supplement ingredient. No proven causality yet.

item.title: The korean telecom giant at the center of anthropic’s mythos controversy
item.description: Days before Anthropic took its most advanced AI models offline, the White House ordered the company to revoke SK Telecom’s access to Claude Mythos over claims of alleged ties to China.

item.title: Vsco launches studio pro mobile photo editing app and plans $500 per year subscription
item.description: VSCO is taking on Adobe with a new Studio Pro editing app rolling out today on iOS and coming to macOS later this year, as Bloomberg reports. At launch, the app offers tools for batch editing, style matching from a reference image, and sharing images through …

item.title: If agents.md smells ripe, your code won’t live up to the hype
item.description: Researchers urge developers to see that less is more when it comes to instructions
